title: Comprehensive motor – accidental damage
text: Accidental damage cover pays for the cost of repairing or replacing your vehicle following sudden, unexpected physical damage caused by an accident, collision, fire, theft, vandalism, or weather event. The standard excess is £250 compulsory plus any voluntary excess you have chosen. Where repair costs exceed the vehicle's current market value, we will declare it a total loss (write-off) and settle at the pre-accident market value less the policy excess and any outstanding finance. Repairs must be carried out at one of our approved repairers to retain the manufacturer warranty. Exclusions: mechanical or electrical breakdown, gradual deterioration, damage whilst the vehicle is being driven by an excluded driver, use for hire or reward, driving under the influence of alcohol or drugs, and competition or motor racing use.

section: Section 5 – Third Party, Fire and Theft
title: Motor – third party liability
text: Third party liability provides indemnity as required by the Road Traffic Act 1988 for your legal liability to third parties arising from the use of your vehicle on public roads. Cover is provided for bodily injury or death to any person (unlimited) and for damage to third party property up to £20,000,000 per occurrence. Legal representation costs are included. You are also covered as a driver of any third party vehicle with the owner's permission on a third party basis only. Exclusions: liability arising from the use of the vehicle as a tool of trade, contractual liability beyond that imposed by statute, and liability between insured persons where they are members of the same household.

title: Motor – windscreen repair and replacement
text: Windscreen, rear screen, sunroof glass and side window repair or replacement is covered without affecting your no-claims discount. Windscreen repair (chip or crack that can be resin-filled): no excess applies. Windscreen replacement: £75 excess if you use our approved glass specialist, or £150 if you arrange your own. The approved glass network includes Autoglass, Belron, and National Windscreens. Advanced driver assistance systems (ADAS) recalibration following windscreen replacement is included. Exclusions: scratches, bodywork surrounding the glass, and damage to mirrors.

section: Section 7 – Personal Accident and Medical Expenses
title: Motor – personal accident benefit
text: Personal accident benefit provides lump sum payments to the policyholder or their spouse/civil partner for accidental bodily injury sustained whilst travelling in or getting in or out of the insured vehicle. Death benefit: £5,000. Loss of limb or sight: £5,000. Permanent total disablement: £5,000. Emergency medical and dental expenses incurred in the UK: up to £500 per person per accident. Benefit is payable regardless of fault and in addition to any third party settlement. Exclusions: suicide or deliberate self-injury, injuries caused whilst under the influence of alcohol or drugs, and pre-existing conditions.

title: Motor – UK and European breakdown cover
text: Roadside breakdown cover provides 24-hour assistance throughout the UK and Europe. UK roadside assistance: attendance within 60 minutes at the roadside or up to 1 hour of labour at the scene. Home start: cover applies if the vehicle breaks down within a quarter mile of your home. National recovery: if the vehicle cannot be repaired at the roadside, we will recover you, your vehicle and up to 7 passengers to a single destination of your choice within the UK. European cover (up to 90 days per trip): local repair, hotel accommodation up to £150 per night for 3 nights, and repatriation of the vehicle if uneconomical to repair abroad. Misfuelling is covered up to £250.

title: Home – buildings cover
text: Buildings insurance covers the cost of repairing or rebuilding your home following sudden and accidental physical loss or damage caused by fire, lightning, explosion, earthquake, storm, flood, subsidence, heave, landslip, theft or attempted theft, vandalism, impact by vehicles or falling trees, and escape of water or oil from fixed domestic systems. The sum insured is the full rebuilding cost index-linked to the BCIS House Rebuilding Cost Index. Accidental damage to underground pipes, drains and cables for which you are responsible is included. Standard buildings excess: £100 (£1,000 for subsidence claims). Contents of outbuildings up to £2,500. Exclusions: coastal or river erosion, damage attributable to poor workmanship, and normal wear and tear.

title: Home – contents cover
text: Contents insurance covers household goods, furnishings, clothing, food and drink, and personal belongings within your home against loss or damage by fire, theft, flood, storm, escape of water, accidental damage, and malicious damage. New-for-old replacement basis applies for items under 5 years old; indemnity basis for older items unless new-for-old extension has been selected. Single article limit: £1,500 (higher-value items must be separately scheduled). Valuables (jewellery, watches, artwork, collectibles) covered up to 33% of contents sum insured. Pedal cycles up to £250 each included. Standard excess: £100. Exclusions: money, securities, business equipment beyond £1,500, and property more specifically insured elsewhere.

title: Home – occupiers and personal liability
text: Occupiers liability and personal liability provide indemnity against your legal liability as occupier of the home and as a private individual for accidental bodily injury to any person or accidental damage to third party property. Limit of indemnity: £2,000,000 any one occurrence. Employers liability for domestic employees (au pairs, cleaners, gardeners): £10,000,000. Legal defence costs are included within the limit of indemnity. Exclusions: liability arising from ownership or use of a motor vehicle, aircraft or watercraft; liability arising from business or professional activities; and liability between members of the same household.

section: Section 3 – Escape of Water and Accidental Damage
title: Home – escape of water and accidental damage
text: Escape of water from fixed domestic plumbing, central heating systems, washing machines, dishwashers, and fish tanks is covered including resultant damage to the structure, fixtures, fittings and contents. Trace and access: we pay the cost of locating the source of the leak, including making good after, up to £5,000. Accidental damage extension (if selected): covers sudden and accidental damage to contents, televisions, computers, and fixed glass in furniture. Gradual leakage, condensation, rot, mould, or damage caused by the insured leaving a tap running are excluded. The first £100 of every escape of water claim is your excess.

title: Private medical – inpatient and day-case
text: Inpatient and day-case treatment at a recognised private hospital or BMI, Spire, Ramsay or Nuffield Health facility is covered in full when referred by your GP and pre-authorised by us. Cover includes: consultant surgeon and anaesthetist fees, operating theatre, pathology and radiology, nursing care, accommodation (standard single en-suite room), and prescribed drugs during stay. Cancer treatment including chemotherapy, radiotherapy, and targeted therapies is covered in full with no monetary limit per policy year. Psychiatric inpatient treatment: up to 28 nights per policy year. Annual benefit limit: £1,000,000. Excess options: £0, £100, £250, £500 or £1,000 per policy year. Exclusions: NHS-available treatment, pre-existing conditions (moratorium or CPME basis), cosmetic procedures, and fertility treatment beyond diagnosis.

title: Private medical – outpatient and diagnostics
text: Outpatient consultations with a consultant specialist following GP referral are covered up to £1,000 per policy year on the Core plan, unlimited on the Comprehensive plan. Diagnostic tests including MRI, CT scan, PET scan, blood tests, and biopsy are covered when ordered by a specialist. Physiotherapy and osteopathy: up to 10 sessions per year on Core plan, 20 on Comprehensive plan, following specialist referral. Mental health outpatient: up to 8 sessions per year with a BACP or UKCP registered therapist or psychiatrist. Optical and dental are not included in this section. Second surgical opinion service is available at no charge through our clinical support team.

title: Private medical – NHS hospital cash benefit
text: NHS cash benefit pays a daily benefit of £100 per 24-hour period for each night you are admitted to an NHS hospital as an inpatient, up to a maximum of 30 nights per policy year. The benefit is paid in addition to any NHS treatment and does not require pre-authorisation. It is designed to help with incidental costs such as parking, travel for visitors, and personal items. The benefit is payable from the second day of admission. Day-case admissions, A&E attendances without overnight admission, and outpatient appointments are not covered under this benefit.

title: Private medical – surgical and specialist fees
text: Surgical fees, anaesthetist fees, and assistant surgeon fees for covered inpatient or day-case procedures are reimbursed at recognised fee guide rates. We follow the Consultant Recognition Programme to ensure quality standards. Theatre charges, prosthetics, and implants for covered procedures are included. For orthopaedic procedures (hip, knee, shoulder replacement) the recognised facility list applies. Robotic-assisted surgery (da Vinci) is covered where the procedure is a recognised alternative to conventional open surgery. Experimental, unproven, or unlicensed treatments are excluded. The insured must obtain pre-authorisation at least 5 working days before elective surgery.

title: Travel – cancellation and trip interruption
text: Trip cancellation reimburses non-refundable prepaid travel, accommodation and excursion costs when the trip must be cancelled before departure due to: serious illness, injury or death of the insured, a travelling companion, or a close relative; redundancy; your home being made uninhabitable; jury service; adverse weather causing closure of your departure airport or port for more than 12 hours; or FCDO advice against travel to the destination issued after the policy was taken out. Curtailment covers the unused proportion of prepaid costs and reasonable additional travel expenses home. Maximum benefit: £5,000 per person. Cancellation due to disinclination to travel, financial failure of a travel provider not protected by ATOL/ABTA, or events known to the insured at time of booking are excluded.

title: Travel – baggage, money and documents
text: Baggage: loss, theft or accidental damage to personal belongings during the trip up to £3,000 in aggregate. Single article limit: £500. Valuables (jewellery, cameras, laptops, tablets): £1,000 in aggregate, only if carried as hand luggage on flights. Delayed baggage: if checked baggage is not delivered within 12 hours of arrival, emergency replacement items up to £200 are reimbursed. Personal money (cash and prepaid cards): £500. Passport and travel documents: £500 for emergency replacement. All loss or theft must be reported to the police or carrier within 24 hours and a written report obtained. Wear and tear, mechanical failure of electronics, and unattended baggage in public places are excluded.
